delivered_note: Route mechanism reconciled in DELIVER (see wave-decisions DWD-06): the read is a GET loader co-located on the /ui-server/sources/:sourceId/uploads resource route, NOT a project/:projectId/source/:sourceId React route. File names below reflect the placeholder plan; the delivered names are in DWD-06.
acceptance_gate: cd ui && npx vitest run app/routes/ui-server/upload-request.router.test.tsx app/routes/ui-server/upload-request.loader.test.ts app/catalog/dataSources/uploadMappers.test.ts app/components/Upload/Upload.test.tsx app/components/Upload/hooks.test.tsx app/__tests__/boundary/no-direct-backend.test.ts
walking_skeleton_scenario: opening an existing source's upload modal invokes the source-uploads route loader (GET /ui-server/sources/:sourceId/uploads), which fetches the source's uploads server-side (through apiFetch, not a browser-direct /api call), maps them via the shared pure mapper, and the modal's Files list renders the persisted history (ingested + pending) oldest-first
context: Surfaced by the DC-155 catalog design review. The upload WRITE saga (createSource -> requestUpload -> putToStorage -> processUpload) is fully behind the BFF, but nothing reads the uploaded-files history back: the inert getSourceUploads read port was removed (commit ae2e641d), and the modal's Files list only ever shows fresh in-session optimistic rows. This slice adds the READ path only.

backend_contract.envelope: JSON:API list of `uploads`; each resource id is the upload_id; attributes: original_filename, file_size, status ('ingested' | 'pending'), row_count (null when pending), created_at (ISO-UTC ms with 'Z')

acceptance_criteria.statement: Opening an existing source's upload modal invokes the source/:sourceId route loader; the modal's Files list renders the source's persisted uploads (ingested + pending) oldest-first, each ingested row showing its row count and a short upload date, each pending row showing no row count.
acceptance_criteria.guard: source-detail.test.tsx :: AC1 (walking skeleton — router navigation renders persisted history) + uploadMappers.test.ts (mapping)
acceptance_criteria.state_at_skeleton: RED — no source/:sourceId route or loader exists; toSourceUploads mapper does not exist; UploadModal.files starts [] and only grows from optimistic uploads
acceptance_criteria.state_final: GREEN — loader fetches via apiFetch, unwrapList + toSourceUploads map the envelope, useLoaderData/useFetcher seeds the modal's Files list

acceptance_criteria.statement: A source with no recorded uploads renders the existing 'No files yet' empty state (an empty-but-OK list is not a failure).
acceptance_criteria.state_final: GREEN — loader resolves [] and the modal keeps its No-files-yet branch

acceptance_criteria.statement: A non-401 upstream read failure degrades gracefully: the modal still opens and the Files list shows no persisted rows (optimistic-only) rather than throwing to an ErrorBoundary; a fresh upload still appears. A 401 redirects to /login (mirrors the app-shell/project loaders).
acceptance_criteria.guard: source-detail.test.tsx :: AC3 (loader read failure -> empty, no throw) + (401 -> redirect)
acceptance_criteria.state_final: GREEN — loader catches the non-401 read failure and returns { uploads: [] }; the modal is unaffected

acceptance_criteria.statement: After the persisted history is seeded, a fresh in-session upload appends AFTER the persisted rows and the earlier rows keep their order (persisted-first ordering).
acceptance_criteria.driving_port: UploadModal Files list (seeded via loader data)
acceptance_criteria.guard: Upload.test.tsx :: AC4 (seeded files + optimistic append order)
acceptance_criteria.state_at_skeleton: RED — modal has no seed prop; files initializes to []
acceptance_criteria.state_final: GREEN — modal initializes files from the seeded SourceUpload[] and appends fresh rows after

acceptance_criteria.statement: The upload-history read introduces no browser-direct /api call: the browser reaches the history only through the same-origin RRv7 loader path; the existing no-direct-backend boundary guard stays green.
acceptance_criteria.driving_port: same-origin RRv7 route loader (server-side apiFetch hop)
acceptance_criteria.guard: no-direct-backend.test.ts (boundary guard remains green with the new read path)
acceptance_criteria.state_at_skeleton: RED if wired browser-side; GREEN once read is server-loader-only
acceptance_criteria.state_final: GREEN — read lands only on the same-origin loader; no credentials:'include' browser fetch to /api

error_path_ratio: 3/5 scenarios are error/edge (empty, read-failure, 401) — meets the >=40% error-coverage bar

steps.title: Skeleton — walking-skeleton acceptance test (RED) + RED scaffolds + roadmap SSOT
steps.intent: Land the walking-skeleton scenario as an honest RED router test and scaffold the two new production modules so imports resolve (RED, not BROKEN): the toSourceUploads mapper and the source-detail loader. Write this DISTILL SSOT + the .feature + wave-decisions. Done-state: ui/ compiles, the acceptance test exists and is RED.
steps.verify: cd ui && npx tsc --noEmit; cd ui && npx vitest run app/routes/source-detail.test.tsx (expect RED)

steps.title: toSourceUploads pure mapper
steps.intent: Add the shared pure mapper JSON:API `uploads` -> SourceUpload[]: name<-original_filename, rows<-row_count (number|null), when<-created_at (short display date), status passthrough ('ingested'|'pending'); preserves oldest-first order. Lives in dataSources/ next to the other mappers so the loader (and any future browser source) map identically.

steps.title: source/:sourceId route + server loader
steps.intent: Add the project-nested route project/:projectId/source/:sourceId with a server loader that fetches GET /sources/{id}/uploads through apiFetch (cookie->Bearer), unwrapList + toSourceUploads. 401 -> redirect('/login'); any other non-2xx is caught and degrades to { uploads: [] } (graceful — NOT an ErrorBoundary throw, unlike the project loader). Register in routes.ts under the project-layout children.
steps.verify: cd ui && npx vitest run app/routes/source-detail.test.tsx

steps.title: Wire the loader data into the upload modal's Files list
steps.intent: On openUpload(existingSource), load the source-detail route via useFetcher().load(`/project/:pid/source/:sourceId`) (keeps the imperative overlay — no modal-routing refactor) and pass the resulting SourceUpload[] into UploadModal. Seed the modal's `files` state from it (persisted-first); widen UploadFile.rows to number|null and render a pending row with no count; keep fresh optimistic rows appending after.
steps.verify: cd ui && npx vitest run app/components/Upload app/routes/source-detail.test.tsx

steps.title: Boundary + convergence gate
steps.intent: Confirm the new read path lands only on the same-origin loader (no browser-direct /api) — reconcile the no-direct-backend guard if needed — and turn on the empty + read-failure scenarios. Run the full ui/ suite as the convergence gate. Zero __SCAFFOLD__ markers remain.
steps.verify: cd ui && npx tsc --noEmit; cd ui && npx vitest run; grep -r "__SCAFFOLD__" ui/app (expect none)

final_gate: commit per step; PR into main (the branch is cut from main, which already carries the merged catalog-behind-bff work — DC-155 #53, etc.; the catalog-behind-bff/release-6 branch is stale, diverged at DC-15 #41, and must NOT be used as the base)
